The Clarus system shall allow access to new surface transportation-related environmental data.

Target allocation code: CAS
Target subsystem: Configuration & Administration Service

The CAS shall support the assignment of access permissions for surface transportation-related environmental data sources.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall collect, quality check, and disseminate environmental data.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Collector Services shall interface with environmental sensors to collect raw environmental data.
The Collector Services shall support multiple sensor types and data formats for environmental data collection.
The Collector Services shall timestamp all collected environmental data with the time of collection.
The Collector Services shall buffer collected environmental data in case of temporary communication failures with downstream services.
The Collector Services shall forward collected environmental data to the Quality Checking Services for validation.
The Collector Services shall log all data collection events, including successes and failures, for audit purposes.
The Collector Services shall handle sensor disconnections and reconnections without data loss.
The Collector Services shall support configurable data collection intervals for each connected sensor.

The Clarus system shall collect, quality check, and disseminate environmental data.

Target allocation code: QEDS
Target subsystem: Qualified Environmental Data Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QEDS shall receive quality-checked environmental data from the QChS.
The QEDS shall store received environmental data in a persistent and queryable format.

The QEDS shall support concurrent access to environmental data without corruption or loss of data.
The QEDS shall log all data retrieval requests and their outcomes for audit purposes.
The QEDS shall validate the format and structure of incoming environmental data before storage.
The QEDS shall provide a mechanism to purge outdated or irrelevant environmental data based on configurable retention policies.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall collect, quality check, and disseminate environmental data.

Target allocation code: QChS
Target subsystem: Quality Checking Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Quality Checking Services shall validate the completeness of incoming environmental data records.
The Quality Checking Services shall perform range checks on all numerical environmental data values against predefined thresholds.
The Quality Checking Services shall detect and flag outliers in environmental data using statistical methods.
The Quality Checking Services shall verify the consistency of environmental data across related parameters.
The Quality Checking Services shall check for temporal consistency in time-series environmental data.
The Quality Checking Services shall apply domain-specific validation rules to environmental data as defined by the system configuration.
The Quality Checking Services shall generate a quality report for each processed environmental data record.
The Quality Checking Services shall assign a quality flag to each environmental data record based on the results of quality checks.
The Quality Checking Services shall log all quality check failures for audit and review purposes.
The Quality Checking Services shall support the configuration of custom quality check rules via the Configuration & Administration Service.

Target subsystem: Watchdog

Draft JSON:

The Watchdog subsystem shall detect an unexpected shutdown of any monitored Clarus system component within 5 seconds of the event occurring.
The Watchdog subsystem shall initiate an automatic recovery procedure for a failed Clarus system component within 10 seconds of detection.
The Watchdog subsystem shall log all detected failures and recovery attempts with timestamps for audit purposes.
The Watchdog subsystem shall provide a heartbeat signal to indicate its own operational status to other Clarus system components.
The Watchdog subsystem shall support configurable recovery actions, including restarting the failed component or notifying system administrators.
The Watchdog subsystem shall ensure that recovery actions do not interfere with the normal operation of other Clarus system components.

The Clarus system shall disseminate environmental metadata in response to polling.

The EMS shall retrieve environmental metadata from the Environmental Metadata Cache (EMC) in response to a polling request.
The EMS shall format the retrieved environmental metadata into a standardized response payload.
The EMS shall return the formatted environmental metadata to the requester within 500 milliseconds of receiving the polling request.
The EMS shall support concurrent polling requests from multiple clients without data corruption.
The EMS shall log each polling request and its corresponding response for audit purposes.
The EMS shall validate the authenticity of the polling request before processing it.
The EMS shall handle and report errors (e.g., invalid request, metadata unavailable) to the requester.

Goal: Assess the drafted target-subsystem LLRs against ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148-inspired requirement quality criteria, then revise them.

Work internally through this checklist for every candidate LLR:
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148-inspired requirement quality criteria:
- Necessary: each LLR is justified by the HLR and target subsystem.
- Correct: each LLR preserves the HLR intent and target subsystem responsibility.
- Unambiguous: each LLR should have one reasonable interpretation.
- Complete enough: each LLR names the responsible system behavior and relevant object.
- Singular/atomic: each LLR expresses one requirement.
- Feasible: each LLR describes implementable behavior.
- Verifiable: each LLR can be checked by test, inspection, demonstration, or analysis.
- Consistent: each LLR does not contradict the HLR or another generated LLR.
- Implementation-independent: each LLR avoids unnecessary design choices.
- Correctly allocated: every returned allocation is exactly the target allocation.
- Uses requirement language: mandatory behavior uses "shall".

Revision rules:
- Keep only LLRs justified by both the HLR and the target subsystem.
- Remove LLRs belonging to another subsystem.
- Split compound LLRs into atomic LLRs only when necessary.
- Remove speculative, duplicate, unverifiable, ambiguous, or implementation-specific LLRs.
- Every returned allocation value must be exactly "QChS".
- Preserve the HLR's intent and modal wording.

Do not reveal private reasoning. Return only the revised final JSON object, with no Markdown or explanatory prose, using exactly this schema:
{"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"QChS","requirement":"requirement text"}]}

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to complete an automated quality checking check of environmental data within ten seconds of data receipt.

Target allocation code: QChS
Target subsystem: Quality Checking Services
